steps.title: Connect methods to cost
steps.content: The passage says longitudinal studies are expensive because they span many years. A public opinion study that only measures current support does not need to follow the same participants across a long period.

steps.title: Apply the passage's logic
steps.content: If the study only asks about current support for a proposal, it can collect the needed information at one point in time. Avoiding a many-year design should also avoid the extra cost that the passage associates with longitudinal research.

steps.title: Reject unsupported comparisons
steps.content: Choice B compares general suitability, Choice C says longitudinal methods would cost less in public opinion research, and Choice D compares reliability. None follows from the passage's cost explanation. The remaining choice draws the relevant cost conclusion. The answer is A.
